_comment: Labeled queries for RAG retrieval evaluation. 'relevant' lists the gold document ids in corpus.json. 'type' marks whether the query shares vocabulary with the gold doc (lexical) or is paraphrased with little word overlap (semantic), so BM25 vs semantic embedders can be compared fairly.

queries.query: what is the price to earnings ratio
how expensive is a stock relative to the profit it makes
compare a company's market price to its net asset value per share
valuation ratio adjusted for expected growth
total value of all of a company's shares
how much cash income a stock pays relative to its price
simple moving average definition
trend line that reacts faster to recent prices
indicator that tells if a stock is overbought or oversold
momentum signal from the difference of two moving averages
bands set a few standard deviations around a moving average
how much a stock moves compared with the whole market
measure of how sharply prices swing over time
reward earned per unit of risk in a portfolio
estimate of the worst loss a portfolio could suffer with some confidence
largest peak to trough drop of an investment
company net profit divided by number of shares
cash left over after operations and capital spending
how efficiently a firm turns shareholder money into profit
ratio of a company's borrowing to its equity
central bank decision that changes the cost of borrowing money
rising prices over time that erode purchasing power
fund that trades on an exchange like a stock and tracks an index
loan to a government or company that pays periodic interest
contract giving the right but not obligation to buy at a strike price
borrowing shares to sell now hoping the price drops
spreading money across many assets to lower risk
investing a fixed amount on a regular schedule regardless of price
order that automatically sells when price falls to a set level
when short term bond rates are higher than long term rates
earning returns on top of previously accumulated returns
restoring a portfolio to its target mix of stocks and bonds
